mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 215) signals weak demand and potential monetary policy accommodation, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate rate cuts.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 215 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > +100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5.0%) signals a tightening labor market, which historically drives consumer spending and equity valuations higher over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where both PAYEMS and UNRATE meet the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO < -2% YoY) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to recessionary risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 600k) and rising real estate finance (RSAFS > 360k) signal robust demand for goods and services, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of cases where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in GDP (GDPC1 < 0.5% QoQ) signals weakening demand, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines below 0.5% QoQ.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE > 0.3pp MoM) combined with declining payrolls (PAYEMS < -50k) signals labor market deterioration, which historically drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of cases where both UNRATE and PAYEMS meet the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) during a high-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL > 220) signals aggressive monetary tightening, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed their thresholds.
